scope: Act 1 EarthBound grammar parity; Swagbound-original content only.

collisionSemantics.routeUse: Solid route object or prop collision; may still y-sort lower-body overlap when visually needed.
Door/warp activation surface tied to a named transition target.
Counter or service barrier where dialogue can happen across collision.
Invisible or subtle route trigger surface owned by a story beat.
Y-sorted route prop that can cover Bosch's lower body while staying readable.
Foreground route prop that can fully cover the player at correct depth.
Reserved Act 1 route-pressure surface; must stay explicit before use.
Reserved water-like or soft-boundary terrain; must stay explicit before use.

mapLayerSemantics.routeUse: Building facades, roofs, and exterior doors that anchor district navigation and enterable locations.
Fences, poles, edge markers, and hard route boundaries that shape traversal without becoming story owners.
Route-readable props, signs, and staged visual cues that point the player through the Act 1 path.
Interactable proof/check objects that own route progress or visible story-state changes.
Item caches and route pickups that must stay visible, inspectable, and tied to objective progression.
Talk/check NPC placements with data-owned movement, interaction ids, and route-beat ownership.
Trees, shrubs, hedges, and organic landmarks that shape route feel while staying subordinate to story owners.
Streetlights, planters, signboards, boxes, and fill props that add density without carrying route progression.

transitionStyles.routeUse: runtime interior exit back to overworld

flagWaivers.reason: Optional Bosch House hallway flavor is not part of the current required Act 1 route spine.
Optional home-threshold flavor exists as authored interior text but is not a required progression gate.
Burger-shop counter flavor is optional town texture and is not required for Act 1 completion.
Fallback interior template records unmodeled optional interiors until each interior has a promoted grammar owner.
Optional mailbox cache claim flag is loot-state bookkeeping, not progression.
Optional mailbox cache claim flag is loot-state bookkeeping, not progression.
Optional repeated-talk memory changes a local NPC line but does not advance route state.
First exterior anomaly line is an optional observation flag and is not a progression gate.

npcDefinitions.label: Biscuit
npcDefinitions.canonSummary: Biscuit grounds the opening in family-house alarm: phones are buzzing, the Route Brief opened while Bosch slept, and the public version problem has already entered the home.

spriteRef.routeUse: Text/speaker owner for the bedroom route brief; no standalone runtime sprite is approved yet.

movementProfile.routeUse: Stationary dialogue owner until a reviewed in-room sprite exists.

npcDefinitions.label: Guardian
npcDefinitions.canonSummary: The Guardian is a practical household logistics voice who treats proof as leaving-the-house preparation: take the charm, sync at the router, and make the public version wait.

spriteRef.routeUse: Interior logistics speaker; future sprite work must preserve practical household authority instead of generic caretaker art.

movementProfile.routeUse: Stationary or tiny in-room idle motion near the household proof/token route.

npcDefinitions.label: Cousin
npcDefinitions.canonSummary: The Cousin reads Signal Town through rumors, stickers, signs, and fake-helpful road language, warning Bosch that paperwork and signal pressure are already watching.

spriteRef.routeUse: Interior rumor/sticker/signal lens; future sprite work must avoid generic hoodie-kid shorthand.

movementProfile.routeUse: Stationary or tiny in-room idle motion near rumor/sticker dialogue.

npcDefinitions.label: Signal-Pet
npcDefinitions.canonSummary: The Signal-Pet is a domestic signal-sensitive threshold keeper, not a robot mascot; it moves aside only when Bosch's Proof-Token Charm matches the person leaving the house.

spriteRef.routeUse: Domestic signal-sensitive threshold owner; future sprite work must avoid robot mascot or literal Wi-Fi iconography.

movementProfile.routeUse: Stationary threshold presence that can imply blocking or moving aside through route state.

npcDefinitions.label: Sidewalk Kid
npcDefinitions.canonSummary: The Sidewalk Kid is the first neighborhood witness, turning yesterday's wrong-Bosch sighting into local testimony before City Hall can formalize it.

spriteRef.routeUse: Overworld first-witness route NPC; any existing runtime placeholder remains review-gated until sprite approval.

movementProfile.routeUse: Small readable sidewalk wander that keeps first-witness interaction discoverable.

npcDefinitions.label: Sal
npcDefinitions.canonSummary: Sal runs the corner-shop proof service with pen-tap pragmatism: mint the Hot Wallet, accept the useful insult, and prepare proof before the Yard.

spriteRef.routeUse: Corner-shop proof-service speaker; future sprite work must read as service-counter pragmatism, not generic shopkeeper art.

movementProfile.routeUse: Stationary counter/service position that supports talk-through-counter interaction.

position.routeUse: Bedroom wake speaker anchored to the Route Brief interaction target.
No-debug route first-witness NPC anchored to the actual overworld proof-map object.

storyObjects.label: Route Brief
storyObjects.canonSummary: The Route Brief is the impossible morning document: already open, externally timestamped, and tied to the Tojiba mint that says Bosch's public version is moving before Bosch is.

storyObjects.label: Side-Room Starter Cache
storyObjects.canonSummary: The Side-Room Starter Cache makes the first kit concrete: backup Route Brief, Practice Bat, and wrong-smile Public Version Card, pairing something solid with something social.

storyObjects.label: Main-Room Threshold
storyObjects.canonSummary: The Main-Room Threshold shifts the route from private house to local witness problem: muted civic alert, misprinted mail, phones holding their buzz, and the sidewalk witness hook.

storyObjects.label: Proof-Token Bowl
storyObjects.canonSummary: The Proof-Token Bowl holds the warm charm that proves the name and sprite leaving the house belong to the same person; it is explicitly not money, weapon, or permission.

storyObjects.label: Receipt Router
storyObjects.canonSummary: The Receipt Router is patched household checkpoint/status hardware, offering Sync and Status as local proof practice instead of a generic save object.

storyObjects.label: Corner-Shop Proof Counter
storyObjects.canonSummary: The Corner-Shop Proof Counter is Signal Town's half-useful proof service: a Tojiba kiosk mints a Hot Wallet and prints an insulting coupon in the same breath.

position.routeUse: pins the public-version Bosch crowd marker to its reviewed proof-map object
pins the park rumor/static marker to its reviewed proof-map object
pins Sal's overworld shop-front read to its reviewed proof-map object
pins Morrow's burger-line overworld read to its reviewed proof-map object
pins the civic gate classifier reader to its reviewed proof-map object
pins the Hot Wallet proof-token marker to its reviewed proof-map object
pins Bonkle's first-witness overworld read to its reviewed proof-map object

interiorBounds.routeUse: Runtime home-hall exit trigger uses the promoted south exit zone for the Act 1 leave-house step.
Signal-pet threshold blocks the promoted front-door exit zone until the Proof-Token Charm is carried.

hotspots.notes: Route beat keeps the pressure-pocket public-version name, but current no-debug route proof activates this obstruction at the civic utility-pole/checkpoint cluster.

battleEncounterRules.routeUse: North park multi-target rumor trial; owns the swarm formation, reward row, and first park clear flag while leaving the park-rumor objective active.
South park single-target trial; owns the second park clear flag and the derived quieted-rumor state that advances the route to Station Gate unlock.
Station Gate boss encounter; owns the Malady stat/reward row, boss defeat state, civic-machine reveal flags, and damaged public-version card reward before refusal processing.

battleActionRules.label: Speaker Weather
battleActionRules.message: The swarm pelts the party with quoted speaker weather: HE SAID IT FIRST, THEY THINK.
battleActionRules.routeUse: North park swarm all-party pressure move; keeps the quoted-speaker rumor readable in the battle message window.

battleActionRules.label: Neon Undertow
battleActionRules.message: The park rumor drags the beat sideways until everybody remembers the shortcut.
battleActionRules.routeUse: North park swarm row pressure move; owns the heavier rumor rhythm without hiding target-shape semantics in battle code.

battleActionRules.label: Phase Siren
battleActionRules.message: A phase siren makes Bosch arrive half a step late to his own explanation.
battleActionRules.routeUse: North park swarm random-target weird move; keeps the unmissable tag and message text explicit for review.

battleActionRules.label: Hot Feedback
battleActionRules.message: The Cinder Cap coughs up feedback that sounds like a witness trying to sound official.
battleActionRules.routeUse: South park single-target pressure move; keeps witness/official language and target grammar tied to the route trial.

battleActionRules.label: Ember Burst
battleActionRules.message: The Cinder Cap throws an ember burst around Bosch's name and calls the scorch a signature.
battleActionRules.routeUse: South park single-target heavy move; owns the signature/scorch metaphor and power ceiling contribution.

battleActionRules.label: Ash Cover
battleActionRules.message: The Cinder Cap smears ash across the route record until the wrong answer looks preprinted.
battleActionRules.routeUse: South park setup move; owns the no-shield-decrement tag so defensive battle behavior stays reviewable.

battleActionRules.label: Static Jab
battleActionRules.message: Malady pricks Bosch with a hostile little name tag and waits for him to answer to it.
battleActionRules.routeUse: Malady boss physical-pressure move; keeps bash-like semantics separate from public-version weirdness.

battleActionRules.label: Public Flare
battleActionRules.message: Malady flares with a public version that sounds almost like Bosch when heard through a waiting room wall.
battleActionRules.routeUse: Malady boss weird move; owns the public-version flare language and message-window fit budget.

battleStatusPanelRules.label: Bosch
battleStatusPanelRules.routeUse: Bosch owns the first battle status card; HP comes from runtime player state, SP uses the fixed Bosch PP row, and rolling HP must remain visible.

battleStatusPanelRules.label: Dripley
battleStatusPanelRules.routeUse: Dripley owns the second battle status card and mirrors the promoted route-party ally row used by Signal Storm and default fallback battles.

battleStatusPanelRules.label: Flexa
battleStatusPanelRules.routeUse: Flexa owns the third battle status card and keeps the fast ally label/HP row readable within the four-card grid.

battleStatusPanelRules.label: Moxie
battleStatusPanelRules.routeUse: Moxie owns the fourth battle status card and anchors the slower high-guts ally row in every Act 1 route battle.

battleTransitionStyles.label: Act 1 touch battle windowing swirl
battleTransitionStyles.routeUse: Route-scale overworld marker contact opens battle through the existing windowing swirl before command input. The style is data-owned so route encounters cannot silently fall back to direct debug battle entry or instant-win behavior.

battleBackdropReadabilityCaps.routeUse: North park route battle backdrop; allow static pressure while keeping command/result/victory windows readable.
South park route battle backdrop; keep the cave cycle slow enough for the single-enemy UI state to stay legible.
Malady boss backdrop; permits a stronger projection effect while capping counter-scroll enough for status cards and victory text.

menuCommandRules.label: Talk to
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Field command window opens Talk as the first command so nearby NPC/object speech is player-owned instead of automatic.

menuCommandRules.label: Goods
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Field command window routes key items and shop goods through the Goods submenu using the promoted Swagbound label.

menuCommandRules.label: Swag
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Field command window keeps Bosch's Swag submenu in the same menu grid while visibility remains tied to available Swag moves.

menuCommandRules.label: Equip
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Field command window shows Equip in the grid while the runtime may gray it out until gear exists.

menuCommandRules.label: Check
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Field command window keeps Check as a deliberate object/route-gate action for proof-token, receipt-router, and gate feedback.

menuCommandRules.label: Status
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Field command window routes objective/status text through the status panel without hiding it in debug-only state.

menuCommandRules.label: Use
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Inventory detail submenu gives usable goods and inspectable key items one primary Use action.

menuCommandRules.label: Give
menuCommandRules.disabledReason: Party transfer is not implemented yet.
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Inventory detail submenu keeps Give visible but disabled for this slice so transfer text does not become an invisible route promise.

menuCommandRules.label: Drop
menuCommandRules.disabledReason: Dropping items is disabled in this slice.
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Inventory detail submenu keeps Drop visible but disabled so route-critical proof items cannot vanish through menu drift.

menuCommandRules.label: Help!
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Inventory detail submenu exposes the Help! description path used to explain key-item and goods text.

menuCommandRules.label: Bash
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Battle command grid keeps the direct attack action in the first slot with the promoted Bash label.

menuCommandRules.label: Swag
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Battle command grid keeps Swag as Bosch's PSI-like action lane without borrowing EarthBound spell names.

menuCommandRules.label: Goods
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Battle command grid routes usable shop goods through the same Goods language as the field menu.

menuCommandRules.label: Defend
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Battle command grid keeps the defensive action in the second row beside Goods.

menuCommandRules.label: Focus
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Battle command grid preserves the Focus support lane in a readable top-row slot.

menuCommandRules.label: Run Away
menuCommandRules.routeUse: Battle command grid keeps Run Away visible as a real battle action while boss encounters still govern whether fleeing resolves.

itemActionRules.routeUse: Route Brief opens as story context in Stuff; it is inspected, not consumed or equipped.
Proof-Token Charm is persistent home proof that unlocks the signal-pet route gate and Receipt Router context.
Practice Bat is starter gear flavor for the route; it stays non-consumable until a later equipment pass promotes gear behavior.
Public Version Card is inspectable false-proof language, not an active use item.
Hot Wallet proof token records Sal's kiosk handoff and stays persistent for civic route checks.
Gate Classifier Receipt is the printed gate result; it unlocks route meaning without being spent.
Damaged Public Version Card is post-Malady evidence and remains inspectable after Act 1.
Pocket Snack is a purchased consumable; buy through the corner-shop menu, use from Goods in battle for HP recovery.
Signal Patch is a purchased battle-pressure guard; it is stackable, consumable, and uses the shield effect.
Focus Fizz is a purchased focus charge; buy through the shop row and use as a consumable battle Goods action.

fontSize.routeUse: Dialogue text scales from the EarthBound presentation window without dropping below 18px.
Field command labels use the promoted menu font scale and stay readable in the compact 3x2 command window.
Field status/objective text remains readable inside the widened status panel.
Inventory detail text carries long key-item descriptions without shrinking below the field-menu baseline.

fontSize.rule: No promoted shop-menu renderer font size; enforce content text budgets until runtime geometry exists.
fontSize.routeUse: Corner-shop menu text stays reviewable without overclaiming a runtime shop-menu font.
Battle command grid labels remain readable in the top command window.
Battle result messages remain readable in the shared message window.
Victory messages use the same readable battle-message font budget.
Four-card HP/status labels stay readable without overlapping the card grid.

objectiveProgressionRules.routeUse: Opening objective is complete when Bosch inspects the bedroom route brief and the wake-up flag enters Act 1 state.
Bedroom exit advances only after the route brief has made the upstairs hall objective visible.
Starter-cache objective completes from the side-room cache and awards the proof tools before Bosch can leave the house.
House exit objective requires the proof-token charm and signal-pet threshold before the overworld route opens.
First-witness objective completes when the neighbor witness gives Bosch a concrete public-version trail.
Proof-preparation objective completes at the corner-shop counter when Sal turns witness evidence into a portable proof object.
Civic brief objective completes at the town authority hotspot and tells the player what the station classifier will count.
Public Version Clique objective completes only after Bosch has both portable proof and the civic brief.
Park-rumor objective is derived from clearing both route battles; the south battle quiets the rumor field after the north flag exists.
Station Gate objective completes when the classifier accepts the proof trail and prints the receipt.
Malady objective completes through the boss victory path, revealing the machine and awarding the damaged public-version card.
Civic refusal objective completes after Malady, preserving Bosch's refusal as the last player-authored route action.
Terminal Act 1 objective appears only after refusal processing sets the final complete flag.

routeActionRules.routeUse: Act 1 starts from the title menu with a normal confirm press, not from a debug scene jump.
Bedroom Route Brief is accepted through an in-room confirm action before the first objective advances.
Bedroom exit is earned by walking through the door path after route-brief confirmation.
Starter cache is confirmed by player input before proof tools enter inventory.
Main-room threshold beat is a player-confirmed home pacing beat before the proof-token gate.
Guardian logistics remains optional, but the no-debug human-pace route now proves Bosch can reach and confirm the household logistics NPC without QA commands.
Cousin rumor/sticker/signal flavor remains optional, but the no-debug human-pace route now proves Bosch can reach and confirm the Cousin NPC without QA commands.
Proof-Token Bowl is explicitly confirmed before the Signal-Pet threshold can clear.
Receipt Router local proof text remains optional and non-blocking, but the no-debug human-pace route now proves Bosch can reach and confirm the patched household object without invoking save/load UX.
Signal-Pet threshold clears through player confirmation after the proof-token charm is collected.
Home exit is a movement/door action after the proof-token and signal-pet beats, not a teleported overworld start.
First witness progress is tied to Talk confirmation at the route NPC.
Corner-shop proof service is confirmed at Sal's counter before proof preparation advances.
Town-gate brief is an overworld Talk action at the civic authority surface.
Public Version Clique obstruction resolves through Check, preserving deliberate player refusal instead of passive proximity.
North park trial enters battle from route-scale encounter contact, then accepts the battle intro by normal input.
South park trial enters battle from route-scale encounter contact after the north park flag exists.
Station classifier unlock is triggered by walking into the gate surface after all proof prerequisites exist.
Malady battle starts only after station unlock and accepts the battle intro with normal input.
Post-boss civic refusal is proven by the no-debug civic-audit milestone before Act 1 completion.
Act 1 completion is proven by the no-debug completion milestone and final state, not by a debug bridge command.

routeSpatialRules.routeUse: No-debug route should enter the proof map at Bosch House's exterior spawn in Home Rise, with the house landmark readable before the first witness walk.
First witness should read as a neighborhood sidewalk stop near the public-version Bosch landmark, not as an abstract objective update.
Corner-shop proof service should resolve from the Main Street shop facade and route NPC cluster before interior dialogue takes over.
Town authority brief should happen inside the civic gate block, using town hall/sign/reader landmarks as the player's map-scale cue.
Current social-obstruction proof is intentionally anchored at the civic utility-pole/checkpoint cluster, matching the promoted no-debug route surface.
North park trial should enter battle from the north-park marker position, preserving map-scale encounter contact.
North park clear should return to the same encounter-marker neighborhood so the victory state reads as route progression, not teleport cleanup.
South park trial should enter from the south-park marker near the trail landmark after north-park state is cleared.
South park clear should keep the player in the park route cell while the objective advances toward Station Gate.
Station classifier unlock should land at the station-gate edge near the boss-gate marker, proving the route is geographically staged before Malady.
Malady should start from the station-gate marker after classifier unlock, preserving route contact before the boss battle.
Post-boss civic audit should resolve inside the station-gate refusal hotspot, keeping the refusal beat tied to a visible map location.
Act 1 completion should remain spatially attached to the station-gate refusal surface rather than only appearing as terminal state.

objectiveStates.text: Wake up and inspect the public-Bosch anomaly in the bedroom.
Leave the bedroom with the Route Brief open.
Collect the side-room proof tools before the public version reaches downstairs.
Take the entry-bowl Proof-Token Charm, clear the signal-pet threshold, and leave Bosch's house.
Find the first witness who saw the other Bosch arrive before you.
Use Sal's shop service to turn the witness trail into portable proof.
Get the civic brief that explains what the Station Gate will count.
Confront the Public Version Clique and refuse their easy Bosch.
Clear the north and south park trials so the proof records your version.
Unlock the Station Gate and keep the classifier receipt it prints.
Face Malady, the fever wearing Bosch's public version.
Refuse civic processing after Malady exposes the machine.
Act 1 is complete. Bosch keeps the damaged public-version proof.

beatOwnershipRules.routeUse: Title/new-game start remains an explicit objective-state beat with status-window and baseline proof ownership.
The waking beat must be anchored to the bedroom route brief object, inspectable text, item/menu ownership, and proof.
Bedroom exit remains a door/warp-owned transition beat, not free-floating story text.
Starter-cache story expansion must keep the cache object, starter item actions, dialogue, and proof linked.
The house threshold is a story object beat that frames the private-to-public route before broad exterior story expansion.
Guardian dialogue must remain anchored to the practical household-logistics NPC instance and canon definition.
Cousin dialogue must remain anchored to rumor/sticker/signal interpretation instead of generic family chatter.
Proof-token acquisition must stay bound to the bowl object, charm item action, route flag, and focused proof.
Signal-pet threshold behavior must remain gate/trigger/dialogue-owned before the player leaves home.
Receipt Router save/status language remains a reviewable home object beat and is not runtime promotion or save/load UX expansion.
Leaving home must be owned by the home-hall exit, route trigger, home-rise sector, action proof, spatial proof, and route flag.
First witness story expansion must stay anchored to a placed witness owner, dialogue pointer, entrance trigger, action proof, and spatial proof.
Corner-shop proof service is a map/shop/object beat with Sal, proof counter, key-item handoff, door trigger, action proof, and corrected interior proof label.
Civic-brief text must hang off the town-authority hotspot, state-gated repeat dialogue, sector, action proof, and spatial proof.
Public-version refusal remains a route gate and hotspot with blocked/success/repeat dialogue and concrete action/spatial proof.
North park trial must keep marker, formation, moves, party panels, transition, backdrop cap, trigger, sector, action/spatial proof, and View Game marker proof together.
South park trial mirrors the north battle ownership while setting the south clear and rumor-quieting route state.
Rumor-field quieting is a route state beat that requires both park clear flags and the south-park completion spatial evidence.
Station classifier unlock must stay a route-gate, dialogue, receipt-item, sector, action, and spatial proof bundle.
Malady must remain a battle marker and boss-encounter bundle with damaged-card reward ownership and View Game marker proof.
Post-boss refusal must stay hotspot/dialogue/objective-owned and select first-refusal text before completion flags are committed.
Act 1 completion must keep terminal objective state, final damaged-card item semantics, route action/spatial evidence, field status, and no-debug human-pace proof linked.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Keep title/menu story copy minimal until full route text expansion has a no-debug pass.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: The route start is proven by no-debug title/menu input, so menu text can be reviewed as part of route grammar rather than a separate story beat.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Expand wake-up and Route Brief text first, because it has no-debug input, owner, dialogue, and flag evidence.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Bedroom route-brief dialogue is safe for route-critical expansion when it stays attached to the registered Route Brief object.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Use this only for short pacing text or door/hall orientation; do not make it carry optional family lore.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Bedroom-to-hall movement is proven by no-debug route input and should remain a navigation beat.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Expand starter-cache item language and Public Version Card framing here before optional house flavor.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Starter-cache dialogue is safe for route-critical expansion because no-debug route input proves the object and item handoff.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Use this as the home-to-street orientation beat after starter-cache language is expanded.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: The main-room threshold is a route-ready home object and can carry concise transition copy.

optionalFlavorContract.proofUse: The no-debug human-pace route now visits and confirms Guardian logistics by keyboard; focused QA remains visual support, not a route-blocking requirement.
optionalFlavorContract.promotionPolicy: May carry optional Guardian dialogue expansion as route-observed flavor; must not become required progression without a separate route-critical gate.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Guardian text can be reviewed as route-observed optional home flavor; keep it non-blocking unless a future route-critical action is approved.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Guardian is a registered optional owner with no-debug keyboard evidence and focused visual proof, while remaining non-blocking household logistics flavor.

optionalFlavorContract.proofUse: The no-debug human-pace route now visits and confirms Cousin rumor/sticker flavor by keyboard; focused QA remains visual support, not a route-blocking requirement.
optionalFlavorContract.promotionPolicy: May carry optional Cousin rumor/lens dialogue expansion as route-observed flavor; must not become required progression without a separate route-critical gate.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Cousin rumor language can be reviewed as route-observed optional home flavor; keep it non-blocking unless a future route-critical action is approved.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Cousin is a canon optional owner with no-debug keyboard evidence and focused visual proof, while remaining non-blocking rumor/sticker/signal flavor.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Expand proof-token language here as the first route-critical identity/proof object beat.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Proof-Token Bowl has no-debug route-log action evidence and focused visual proof, so it can safely carry expanded route-critical proof language.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Expand threshold language after Proof-Token Bowl, keeping it domestic and signal-sensitive rather than literal network iconography.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Signal-Pet Gate is route-ready because no-debug input proves confirmation before home exit.

optionalFlavorContract.proofUse: The no-debug human-pace route now visits and confirms Receipt Router local-proof text by keyboard; focused QA remains visual support, and save/load UX remains out of scope.
optionalFlavorContract.promotionPolicy: May carry optional Receipt Router local-proof/status flavor as route-observed text; must not introduce required save/load UX or route blocking in this pass.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Receipt Router wording can be reviewed as route-observed optional home flavor; keep save/load UX and required progression out of scope.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Receipt Router is a canon optional object with no-debug keyboard evidence and focused visual proof, while remaining non-blocking local-proof/status hardware.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Use for a short route-pacing line only after required home object text is stable.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Home exit is no-debug route-ready and proves the route leaves the house only after the proof-token and signal-pet gate.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Expand witness testimony after home route-critical text, keeping it anchored to the sidewalk witness owner.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: First witness is route-ready through no-debug action and spatial evidence.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Expand Tojiba/corner-shop proof service copy after witness text.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Corner-shop proof service is route-ready and has corrected proof-label ownership.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Expand civic briefing only within the town-authority hotspot and state-gated repeat dialogue.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Town Gate Brief is route-ready through no-debug hotspot action and spatial evidence.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Expand refusal dialogue after civic briefing, keeping the activation surface tied to the current civic utility-pole/checkpoint cluster.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Social obstruction is route-ready through action/spatial evidence, while focused QA proof remains diagnostic support.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Keep story expansion to battle intro/result language already covered by battle UI readability checks.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: North park trial is route-ready through battle marker, action, and spatial clear evidence.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Keep story expansion to battle intro/result language and south-park route consequence text.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: South park trial is route-ready through battle marker, action, and spatial clear evidence.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Use as a state-transition beat only; put actual player-facing text on the surrounding battle/result/gate owners.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Rumor-field quieting is route-ready state evidence, not a standalone dialogue surface.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Expand classifier receipt/failure copy after park route text is stable.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Station classifier is route-ready and must stay tied to the station gate edge and receipt item semantics.

storyExpansionReadinessRules.recommendedNextStep: Keep boss story expansion inside battle message/victory and damaged-card reward surfaces.
storyExpansionReadinessRules.routeUse: Malady is route-ready through boss marker, battle action, and station-gate spatial evidence.
